title: Best VPN for Torrenting in 2026: Fast, Secure & P2P-Friendly
description: Looking for the best VPN for torrenting in 2026? We tested the top options for speed, no-logs policies, and P2P support.
content:
What Makes a VPN Good for Torrenting?

Not all VPNs allow torrenting, and of those that do, only a handful are fast and private enough to actually use for P2P. Three things matter most:

P2P-optimized servers. The best torrenting VPNs route P2P traffic through dedicated servers configured for high throughput and stable connections. Generic servers often throttle BitTorrent traffic or block it outright.

A verified no-logs policy. A VPN that logs your IP address and timestamps is useless for privacy. The most trustworthy providers have been independently audited by security firms like Cure53 or KPMG, and some have had their no-logs policies tested in court when authorities requested user data.

Kill switch. If the VPN drops unexpectedly, your real IP address is exposed to the torrent swarm. A working kill switch blocks all internet traffic the moment the VPN disconnects, preventing any leak before the connection restores.

The 6 Best VPNs for Torrenting in 2026

1. NordVPN — Best Overall for Torrenting

NordVPN is the most consistently recommended VPN for torrenting in 2026. It has 6,000+ servers across 111 countries, with dedicated P2P servers in every major region. The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard-based) delivers speeds that rarely drop below 400 Mbps on nearby servers, more than enough to saturate a gigabit home connection.

The no-logs policy has been audited three times by PricewaterhouseCoopers and once by Deloitte. In 2022, Dutch authorities requested NordVPN user data in a criminal case. NordVPN had nothing to hand over. SOCKS5 proxy support is also included, which allows routing just your torrent client through the VPN without affecting other apps.

P2P servers: Yes, dedicated
Protocol: NordLynx (WireGuard)
Price: from $3.99/month (2-year plan)
Simultaneous connections: 10

2. ExpressVPN — Best for Speed

ExpressVPN consistently leads speed tests across all categories. The Lightway protocol achieves faster reconnection times than WireGuard when switching between networks. All servers support P2P. The no-logs policy was tested in 2017 when Turkish authorities seized an ExpressVPN server during a murder investigation and found no useful data.

P2P servers: All servers
Protocol: Lightway
Price: from $8.32/month (yearly plan)
Simultaneous connections: 8

3. Private Internet Access — Best for Power Users

PIA lets you configure encryption level, tunneling protocol, port forwarding settings, and DNS from the desktop client. Port forwarding allows incoming connections from the torrent swarm, improving peer discovery and download speeds on heavily seeded torrents. PIA is one of the few consumer VPNs that still supports port forwarding.

The no-logs claim was tested twice in US court cases. The FBI subpoenaed PIA and received nothing both times.

P2P servers: All servers with port forwarding
Protocol: WireGuard or OpenVPN
Price: from $2.03/month (3-year plan)
Simultaneous connections: Unlimited

4. Mullvad — Best for Anonymous Payment

Mullvad does not require an email address to create an account. You receive a random 16-digit account number. Payment can be made by cash sent in an envelope, Bitcoin, Monero, or credit card. For users who want the most anonymous possible VPN relationship, Mullvad is in a category of its own.

All servers support P2P. WireGuard is the default. Kill switch is always-on. At 5 euros per month flat with no multi-year discount, Mullvad costs more than NordVPN or PIA but offers something those services cannot: a provider that genuinely knows nothing about who you are.

P2P servers: All servers
Protocol: WireGuard or OpenVPN
Price: 5 euros/month flat
Simultaneous connections: 5

5. Surfshark — Best for Unlimited Devices

Surfshark's unlimited simultaneous connections policy means you can run it on every device in your home without hitting a cap. P2P is supported on specific servers clearly labeled in the app. WireGuard is available on all platforms. For households with multiple users all torrenting on different devices, Surfshark is the most cost-effective option.

P2P servers: Dedicated P2P servers
Protocol: WireGuard
Price: from $2.49/month (2-year plan)
Simultaneous connections: Unlimited

6. ProtonVPN — Best Open Source Option

ProtonVPN is operated by Proton AG, the company behind ProtonMail. The VPN app is open source and publicly audited. Switzerland has strong privacy laws and sits outside the Five Eyes and Fourteen Eyes intelligence-sharing agreements. P2P is supported on Plus plan servers.

P2P servers: Plus plan servers
Protocol: WireGuard
Price: from $4.99/month (Plus)
Simultaneous connections: 10

Key Features for Torrenting Explained

SOCKS5 Proxy

A SOCKS5 proxy lets you route only your torrent client through the VPN while other traffic uses your regular connection. This avoids full encryption overhead and can improve torrent speeds. NordVPN and PIA both include SOCKS5 proxies. Trade-off: SOCKS5 provides IP masking but not encryption.

Port Forwarding

Port forwarding opens an inbound connection port through the VPN tunnel, allowing peers in the torrent swarm to connect to you directly. This improves upload speed and helps with ratio requirements on private trackers. PIA is the most prominent consumer VPN that still offers port forwarding in 2026.

Split Tunneling

Split tunneling lets you choose which apps go through the VPN and which use your regular connection. Configure your torrent client to always use the VPN while streaming or browsing goes through your normal connection, keeping speeds high for non-P2P traffic.

DNS Leak Test: Do This Before Torrenting

Before torrenting with a VPN, visit dnsleaktest.com with the VPN connected and run the extended test. If results show your ISP's DNS servers, the VPN is leaking DNS queries and your ISP can see which domains you are resolving. All six VPNs on this list pass DNS leak tests under normal conditions, but verify on your specific device.

Which VPN Should You Choose?

For most users: NordVPN offers the best balance of speed, price, verified no-logs, and ease of use. For power users who want port forwarding: PIA. For maximum anonymity: Mullvad. For unlimited devices on a budget: Surfshark.
